pending.body: `classify_running_snapshot_liveness` now treats any running snapshot with no `run.pid` as stale (`src/cli/run.rs:1518-1535`), and `run resume` / `run stop` immediately reclaim that owner in the stale path (`src/cli/run.rs:1833-1900`, `src/cli/run.rs:2007-2045`). That breaks mixed-version rollouts for daemon-owned runs: daemon tasks that were already in flight before this change have no pid file yet, so the new binary will misclassify a still-live daemon run as stale and can steal its writer lock. Add a compatibility path for preexisting daemon runs instead of treating missing pid as stale unconditionally.

Stale daemon recovery now deletes the observed `WorktreeLease` directly in `reclaim_specific_project_writer_owner` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:119-152`), and `run resume` / `run stop` call that path before recovering the snapshot (`src/cli/run.rs:1875-1900`, `src/cli/run.rs:2013-2044`). That bypasses the daemon reconcile/release flow that marks the daemon task failed and clears `task.lease_id` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:473-491`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:601-608`). `DaemonTaskService::retry_task` still rejects tasks with a retained lease reference (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:499-505`), so a crashed daemon task can be left permanently wedged after CLI recovery. Route stale daemon recovery through the normal daemon cleanup path or clear/update the daemon task record alongside the lease reclamation.

`run stop` still has a PID-reuse race. `send_signal_to_pid` checks `FileSystem::is_pid_alive(record)` and then sends `kill()` to the raw PID (`src/cli/run.rs:1624-1638`), and the stop flow uses that helper for both SIGTERM and SIGKILL (`src/cli/run.rs:2055-2064`). If the orchestrator exits after the liveness check and the PID is recycled before `kill()` runs, `run stop` can signal an unrelated process. The current `proc_start_ticks` check is only a userspace preflight; it does not make the signal delivery itself safe. Use an OS handle that remains bound to the original process (for example a pidfd on Linux) or another atomic mechanism before claiming PID-reuse is handled.

The patch is metadata-only and does not implement the requested run-lifecycle hardening contract in runtime code. The only file-level changes are in `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/journal.ndjson` (new `reviewer_started` entries at lines 113-114) and `.ralph-burning/projects/rlm/run.json` (added `stage_resolution_snapshot`/metadata under `active_run` at lines 16-37), while the behavior files required by the goal (`src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/cli/run.rs`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs`) are untouched by this diff.
